channel.title: MATEKHET - A Machine Music Podcast
channel.description: In-depth analysis, discussion, and interviews from Machine Music (MachineMusic.net) about anything and everything metal, hardcore, and other forms of extreme music.
channel.author: Machine Music
channel.itunes:author: Machine Music
channel.itunes:summary: In-depth analysis, discussion, and interviews from Machine Music (MachineMusic.net) about anything and everything metal, hardcore, and other forms of extreme music.

item.title: MATEKHET - A Machine Music Podcast - Ep. 4 - Church of Authenticity
item.description:
In this episode we revisit the interview with hardcore/post-hardcore legend Ian MacKaye (Ep. 3) and introduce a new context into the discussion of authenticity and "realness" in hardcore - the shift in spirituality and religiosity in 17th and 18th century England and the United States. We pivot away from a discussion of authenticity in terms of exposing truth or "feeling real" toward a discussion of an authenticity of "mystery," including a snippet from a recent interview with Kristoffer Rygg of Norwegian experimental band Ulver. 

Closing Track - An Isolated Mind - "Afraid of Dissonance" (2019). 

Full Rygg Interview: https://bit.ly/2y8e8SV

Patreon: https://bit.ly/3eFHTel

Facebook: https://bit.ly/3dX0Acp

Instagram: https://bit.ly/2zNHSVx
item.dc:creator: Machine Music
item.itunes:summary:
In this episode we revisit the interview with hardcore/post-hardcore legend Ian MacKaye (Ep. 3) and introduce a new context into the discussion of authenticity and "realness" in hardcore - the shift in spirituality and religiosity in 17th and 18th century England and the United States. We pivot away from a discussion of authenticity in terms of exposing truth or "feeling real" toward a discussion of an authenticity of "mystery," including a snippet from a recent interview with Kristoffer Rygg of Norwegian experimental band Ulver. 

Closing Track - An Isolated Mind - "Afraid of Dissonance" (2019). 

Full Rygg Interview: https://bit.ly/2y8e8SV

Facebook: https://bit.ly/3dX0Acp

Instagram: https://bit.ly/2zNHSVx

item.title: MATEKHET - A Machine Music Podcast - Ep. 3 - An Interview with Ian MacKaye
item.description:
Our discussion of the concept of “authenticity” in heavy music continues with a brand-new interview with the legendary Ian MacKay (Minor Threat, Teen Idles, Embrace, Fugazi, The Evens, Coriky, Dischord Records). We discuss the various forms authenticity has taken in punk and hardcore, and what Ian considers to be “real” music.

Closing song: Karkait - “Karkait Ha'Kiyum”

https://karkait.bandcamp.com/

Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/machinemusic
item.dc:creator: Machine Music
item.itunes:summary:
Our discussion of the concept of “authenticity” in heavy music continues with a brand-new interview with the legendary Ian MacKay (Minor Threat, Teen Idles, Embrace, Fugazi, The Evens, Coriky, Dischord Records). We discuss the various forms authenticity has taken in punk and hardcore, and what Ian considers to be “real” music.

Closing song: Karkait - “Karkait Ha'Kiyum”

https://karkait.bandcamp.com/

Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/machinemusic

item.title: MATEKHET - A Machine Music Podcast - Ep. 2 - Sonic Authenticity.
item.description: Continuing down the path of "authenticity" in metal and extreme music we revisit an interview with Johannes Persson (Cult of Luna), and dissect SUMAC's monolithic song "Thorn in the Lion's Paw" via Clement Greenberg's critique of abstract expressionism and Charles Peirce philosophy of language. Yup.  For the full interview with Johannes: shorturl.at/FJ459  MATEKHET on Soundcloud: https://bit.ly/2RSoiOcMATEKHET on Anchor: https://bit.ly/2KpIyCC Support Machine Music on Patreon: https://bit.ly/3eFHTelFollow Machine Music on Facebook: MachineMusic1981 Follow Machine Music on Instagram: @MachineMusic1981 Follow Machine Music on Spotify: https://spoti.fi/34xmorp All rights to  "Thorn in the Lion's Paw" belong to SUMAC and Profound Lore Records.
item.dc:creator: Machine Music
item.itunes:summary: Continuing down the path of "authenticity" in metal and extreme music we revisit an interview with Johannes Persson (Cult of Luna), and dissect SUMAC's monolithic song "Thorn in the Lion's Paw" via Clement Greenberg's critique of abstract expressionism and Charles Peirce philosophy of language. Yup.  For the full interview with Johannes: shorturl.at/FJ459  MATEKHET on Soundcloud: https://bit.ly/2RSoiOcMATEKHET on Anchor: https://bit.ly/2KpIyCC Support Machine Music on Patreon: https://bit.ly/3eFHTelFollow Machine Music on Facebook: MachineMusic1981 Follow Machine Music on Instagram: @MachineMusic1981 Follow Machine Music on Spotify: https://spoti.fi/34xmorp All rights to  "Thorn in the Lion's Paw" belong to SUMAC and Profound Lore Records.

item.title: MATEKHET - A Machine Music Podcast - Ep. 1 - AUTHENTICITY IN EXTREME MUSIC
item.description: In this episode we explain the rationale and motivation behind the MATKHET podacst, and introduce our first topic of discussion: "authenticity." We touch on the different kinds of authenticity, the centrality of "authentic" emotion and expression in metal and hardcore, and include clips that expand on those issues from past interviews with Greg Anderson (Sunn O))), Goatsnake, Burning Witch, Southern Lord Recordings) and Aaron Weaver (Wolves in the Throne Room).
item.dc:creator: Machine Music
item.itunes:summary: In this episode we explain the rationale and motivation behind the MATKHET podacst, and introduce our first topic of discussion: "authenticity." We touch on the different kinds of authenticity, the centrality of "authentic" emotion and expression in metal and hardcore, and include clips that expand on those issues from past interviews with Greg Anderson (Sunn O))), Goatsnake, Burning Witch, Southern Lord Recordings) and Aaron Weaver (Wolves in the Throne Room).
